sentence_reviews.text: Coordinates, phases, or learned positions inject order, time, or location into models whose base computation is otherwise permutation-equivariant or location-agnostic.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Vaswani et al. 2017, Section 3.5 on positional encodings; NeRF paper/reference for coordinate-based neural fields; PML general representation-learning context.
sentence_reviews.notes: Transformer positional encodings and coordinate-based fields support the idea that order/location is injected through extra encodings. The broader phrasing over time, phase, and location-agnostic base computations is a synthesis rather than a single-source statement.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Sequences, events & time series, Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Transformer Section 3.5 describes positional encodings because sequence models need token order information.
sentence_reviews.notes: Sequence order is directly relevant, but the rationale wording is graph-derived.

sentence_reviews.text: Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings consumes, emits, or organizes information using Embeddings & distributed representations.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Transformer Section 3.5 adds positional encodings to input embeddings; PML covers learned representations generally.
sentence_reviews.notes: Adding positional encodings to embeddings is directly supported for transformers; the more general relation is a synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings is modeled as depending on Sequences, events & time series, Embeddings & distributed representations and feeding downstream nodes such as Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms, Neural fields & radiance-field models.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.

edge_reviews.notes: Sequence order is a core motivation for positional encodings in transformers. Direction and type are defensible.
Transformer positional encodings are combined with token embeddings, supporting a core representation relation.
